summary: Run a fine weak-pi pulsed ODMR frequency refinement on accepted r03 after the first Ramsey scout produced weak non-claim-grade spectral content and a carrier mismatch.
scientific_rationale: The first Ramsey scout completed and is analyzable, but the strongest exploratory combined oscillatory component is near 0.884 MHz rather than the programmed 1.5 MHz carrier, and the stored averages disagree. The previous weak-pi pODMR grid step was 1 MHz and deliberately treated as grid-limited. A fine weak-pi pODMR is a targeted center-frequency diagnostic before a longer/higher-SNR Ramsey repeat, not a blind Ramsey repeat. The fine scan uses 3.8745..3.8775 GHz in 31 points (0.1 MHz spacing), 4 averages x 50000 repetitions, mod_depth=0.1 and length_rabi_pulse=0.57 us. The prior weak-pi dip was about 14.3% / 6.42 kcps, about 3.9x the median signal SEM at the same shot budget, so the feature should remain visible if the center has not drifted far outside the narrow window.
comparison_targets: NV_RESEARCH_KNOWLEDGE Experiment Defaults: weak-pi pODMR center before Ramsey; Shot Budget/Data Quality: avoid blind Ramsey repeats when first scout non-claim-grade
expected_evidence: terminal savedexperiment and raw readout export for fine weak-pi pODMR
raw/readout-aware center review before choosing a Ramsey repeat frequency
success_criteria: bridge execute completes with nonzero acquired data
raw signal/readout-aware views show a resonance-scale dip within the fine window
center estimate is supported enough to choose mw_freq for a redesigned Ramsey repeat or to identify a drift/tracking/frequency failure mode
no tracking/count-collapse/hardware anomaly invalidates the data
stop_conditions: bridge queue becomes occupied before submission
verifier or MATLAB advisory blocks execute
RunExperiment aborts or returns zero averages/no saved data
bridge_action: execute managed single-item batch for pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1 fine weak-pi pODMR

planned_measurement.analysis_plan: After terminal, raw/readout-aware pODMR review first: raw signal, point-wise ratio, fitted-reference-line normalization, per-average minima, and scan-order-aware drift. Use a center downstream only if raw signal presence is supported.

model_and_resolvability.coverage_reason: The +/-1.5 MHz window around 3.876 GHz covers either sign of the ~0.6 MHz Ramsey carrier mismatch and gives 0.1 MHz grid spacing, much finer than the previous 1 MHz grid.
model_and_resolvability.distinguishability: Keeping 4 x 50000 shots should keep the prior weak-pi dip at about 3.9 SEM on the raw signal channel, enough to localize the minimum if tracking remains stable.

warnings: sequence manifest pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1 is in staging status and should stay limited to validate/dry_run until promoted.
